name: multi_replace
description: Perform multiple string replacements or structured file edits (replace, insert, delete, append, prepend, insert-at-line, insert-file) in one step, with optional Python transform.

parameters.name: replace <old> <new>
parameters.description: Replace all occurrences of old with new

parameters.name: insert-before <pattern> [<text>]
parameters.description: Insert text before matching line; reads stdin if text omitted

parameters.name: insert-after <pattern> [<text>]
parameters.description: Insert text after matching line; reads stdin if text omitted

parameters.name: insert-at-line <N> [<text>]
parameters.description: Insert text at line N (1-indexed); reads stdin if text omitted

parameters.name: insert-file-at-line <N> <path>
parameters.description: Insert content from file at line N

parameters.name: insert-file-before <pattern> <path>
parameters.description: Insert file content before matching line

parameters.name: insert-file-after <pattern> <path>
parameters.description: Insert file content after matching line

parameters.name: append <text>
parameters.description: Append text to end of file

parameters.name: prepend <text>
parameters.description: Prepend text to beginning of file

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses multiple sequential sed/grep/Python edits on the same file into one step, saving N-1 agent steps and command tokens; insert-at-line replaces head-N+cat pipeline pattern with a single command; insert-file-* actions eliminate temp file + head/cat patterns seen in samples.
